source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Strategic Ladder Validator
thesis: Validate a proposed NVDA entry ladder against strike pressure, IV/HV context, pre-market distance, fill plausibility, and participant-pressure logic before orders remain active.
notes: Best explicit module candidate in the corpus. Suitable as a pre-open execution sanity gate.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Volatility Sentiment Index
thesis: Use VXX move, VIX term structure, implied-volatility change, and macro stress posture to elevate or suppress fill aggression in volatile conditions.
notes: Looks more like a sizing/risk overlay than a standalone alpha signal.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: OBV VI Flow Confirmation
thesis: Use OBV and Volume Index behaviour to confirm whether sell-side or buy-side control is real rather than a fake momentum pulse.
notes: The corpus describes this as execution-grade flow confirmation rather than a full standalone strategy.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Compression Regime Detector
thesis: Detect low-momentum compression / distribution zones where breakout chasing should be blocked and mean-revert logic should dominate.
notes: Strong candidate for a runtime veto module.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Options Behaviour Cluster
thesis: Summarise IV-curve slope, OI-cluster width, and skew divergence to frame gamma walls, magnet zones, and boundary expectations.
notes: This is likely a feature family plus a summarising signal layer rather than one monolithic runtime module.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Macro Adaptive Weighting Filter
thesis: Overweight or suppress particular signal families depending on whether the market is in risk-off panic, rangebound macro uncertainty, or reversal-event conditions.
notes: This is a meta-module / global overlay and belongs near the risk gateway.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Overnight Carry Evaluator
thesis: Decide whether to leave capital engaged overnight, whether to keep limit orders active, and how much exposure is justified given volatility state, carry risk, and pre-market/Asia conditions.
notes: One of the clearest missing artefacts in the current repo.

source_document: My tier 1 desk Lol not GS (1).pdf
name: Asia Precursor Context Filter
thesis: Use Asia-session market context (Japan, Hong Kong, China index futures, rates/FX spillover) as a bounded precursor overlay for U.S. pre-market NVDA context.
notes: Should stay as a context filter rather than a dominant directional signal.
